runner: hive (alfred); 3 XIAO ESP32-S3 capturing, hearing all 9-board mesh heartbeats

args.rule: stationary node RSSI-σ < 5 dBm over settled window → Infrastructure

expectations.observed: 49/49 settled (n>=20) neighbour-link readings had σ < 5 dBm; σ min=0.19, max=3.94, mean=0.94 dBm; 0 readings ≥5 dBm (0.0%). All settled stationary links classified Infrastructure.

notes: FINDING B's predicted refutation (stationary multipath/AGC/integer-dBm jitter → σ>5dBm → INFRA misclassified MOBILE) did NOT reproduce on this static co-located bench: worst stationary link (cross-room DFR 2cab5f69) σ=3.94 dBm = ~1 dBm headroom. MARGIN OBSERVATION (not a refutation, route to specs as robustness input): the thin ~1 dBm headroom supports the already-flagged §2.4 fix (σ threshold + hysteresis + stationary-noise margin + per-transport mapping) — a noisier RF environment (more nodes/interference/longer links) could plausibly exceed 5 dBm. SETTLE NOTE: low-sample neighbours (n<20) default Mobile (firmware settle-floor) = expected conservative behavior; classification needs sufficient traffic to settle (relevant to §2.4's >10min window). RSSI-UNIT CAVEAT: esp-radio rx_control.rssi reads positive (~191-246); σ is offset-invariant so the dBm verdict holds, but the absolute representation should be confirmed if absolute-RSSI thresholds (not just σ) are ever used.
